full_text:
GeoMX data analysis, meeting 6

20.08.2025
Nika Mikhailava

Agenda

Integrate Batch 3 into the existing dataset and perform exploratory analysis 
Differential Gene Expression (DGE) comparisons between stroma and TSI in individual samples 
Clustering of the most variable genes in stroma and TSI compartments 
Analysis of compartment-specific gene expression in stroma and TSI 
Sanity check and validation of findings

Are the cell proportions in stroma and TSI different?

immune landscape is spatially remodeled in stroma and TSI
functional role change of fibroblasts at the TSI is due to qualitative changes, not due to quantitative

Are TSI and stroma segments evenly represented across the dataset?

Are TSI and stroma segments evenly represented across paired samples?

Which genes are differentially expressed in fibroblasts between TSI and stroma?

DGE comparisons where done between the TSI and Stroma of the individual patients.
DEG intersecting between at least two patients were selected. 
Selected genes are not primarily associated with fibroblasts

Sanity check: compare fibroblasts gene expression with tumor gene expression from nearby tumor compartment

How can clustering using the most variable genes be applied to distinguish between TSI and stroma regions in the dataset?

Do fibroblasts express the most variable genes?

Compare expression of selected genes from fibroblasts in the TSI-stroma compartment with gene expression of tumor cells in the adjacent TSI-tumor compartment.

Conclusions from Matt-Whitney comparisons of most variable genes expressions

Stroma-enriched CAFs (matrix-constructive phenotype):
ECM production: COL1A1, COL3A1, FN1, TNC, DPT, MGP 
Metabolism/differentiation: ADH1B, ADIPOQ, AKR1C2, HSD11B1, FMO2 
Chemokines/immune crosstalk: CCL19, CXCL1, CXCL3, CXCL14, PF4, PPBP 
Functional profile: ECM construction, immune recruitment, metabolic specialization 
TSI-enriched CAFs (activated, tumor-promoting phenotype):
Wnt pathway modulation: SFRP2, SFRP4 
Stress/immune response: IFI6, ISG15, DUSP1, EGR1 
Angiogenesis/remodeling: THBS1, TIMP3, MMP 
Functional profile: Activated, stress-responsive, angiogenic niche shapers
But! Some genes related to cell adhesion and matrix deposition also upregulated COL1A1, COL3A1, FN1,

Distant stroma CAFs: 
Matrix-constructive, metabolically specialized, immune-recruiting 
Likely support tumor growth via ECM deposition and immune modulation 
TSI CAFs: 
Activated, stress-responsive, pro-angiogenic 
Shape the tumor invasive front and aid therapy resistance

Approach

CAF clusters were annotated based on CAF genes presence (aSMA, VIM, FAP, PDGFRA/B, POSTPN)

Findings: (STS=2 and LTS=2)
CAFs in STS tumors expressing many canonical fibroblasts markers (αSMA, VIM, PDGFRα/β, FAP, S100A4)
CAFs in LTS tumors expressing mainly mainly αSMA, VIM fibroblasts markers (myofibroblasts) 
CD36 (in stroma) and periostin (in TSI) enriched in STS samples in CAFs

Different fibroblasts-tumor crosstalks found in LTS and STS samples 
STS-specific interactions: APOE–LRP5, THBS2–CD47, WNT10A–FZD5/7, TGFB1–ENG/ACVRL1, IGF1–IGF1R

Next steps:

Select the number of genes for variability analysis. 
Perform variance estimation for STS and LTS samples. 
Integrate spatial transcriptomics (ST) data with scRNA-seq data. 
Apply GSEA to identify key biological processes in TSI versus stroma fibroblasts.

Multimodal intersection analysis

Moncada, R., Barkley, D., Wagner, F. et al. Integrating microarray-based spatial transcriptomics and single-cell RNA-seq reveals tissue architecture in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas. Nat Biotechnol 38, 333–342 (2020). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41587-019-0392-8
